engineIntelligence.h2: Suzuki Landy Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Suzuki Landy in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Suzuki Landy M16A engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure - OEM 18210-85J00 wears prematurely, causing rattle on cold start.
Head gasket failure - TSB ENG-08-03 applies; coolant loss and white smoke common after 80k miles.

cards.altText: Suzuki Landy K15B engine - petrol 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR valve carbon buildup - valve 13750-85Z00 cokes up, causing rough idle and reduced performance.
Intake manifold carbon deposits - common on high-mileage DD51W units with short-journey use.

cards.altText: Suzuki Landy G13B engine - petrol 1.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing belt failure - interference engine; belt snap causes catastrophic valve damage if not replaced every 60k miles.
Piston ring wear - oil consumption increases on high-mileage microvan units.

engineGuide.h2: Suzuki Landy Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Suzuki Landy across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: The G13B powered the Every Landy microvan (1999-2005), an interference engine requiring strict timing belt maintenance.
compatibleVariants: Every Landy HE, Every Landy HE22, Every Landy 1.3G, Every Landy 1.3X
commonFailures: Timing belt failure - interference engine; snap causes bent valves if not replaced every 60k miles
Piston ring wear - oil consumption increases on high-mileage units after 90k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for G13B Suzuki Landy engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The M16A became the backbone of the DA64W Landy range, offering balance of economy and reliability with known tensioner issues.
compatibleVariants: DA64W Landy 1.6G, DA64W 1.6X, DA64W JS, DA64W JX, DA64W 2WD, DA64W 4WD
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure - OEM 18210-85J00 wears, causing cold-start rattle; TSB SB-ENG-2013-07 applies
Head gasket failure - TSB ENG-08-03; coolant loss and white smoke common after 80k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for M16A Suzuki Landy engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: The larger-displacement M18A offered more torque for 4WD and higher-spec DA64W Landy variants.
compatibleVariants: DA64W Landy 1.8G, DA64W 1.8X, DA64W GLX, DA64W 4WD variants
commonFailures: Oil pump relief valve failure - part 15100-85J00 can stick, causing low oil pressure warnings
Head gasket weakness - shares M16A pattern; MLS gasket upgrade recommended in rebuilds
entries.cta: Get quotes for M18A Suzuki Landy engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: The K15B powers the DD51W Landy generation, featuring updated emissions control but prone to EGR carbon issues.
compatibleVariants: DD51W Landy 1.5G, DD51W 1.5X, DD51W JS, DD51W JL, DD51W GLX, DD51W 2WD/4WD
commonFailures: EGR valve carbon buildup - valve 13750-85Z00 cokes up, causing rough idle and performance loss
Intake manifold carbon deposits - common on high-mileage units with short-journey urban use
entries.cta: Get quotes for K15B Suzuki Landy engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Mild Hybrid Petrol
entries.history: The K15C introduces mild-hybrid technology to the DD52W Landy, with an integrated starter-generator for efficiency.
compatibleVariants: DD52W Landy 1.5 Hybrid G, DD52W Hybrid X, DD52W Hybrid GLX, DD52W Hybrid 4WD
commonFailures: ISG belt failure - integrated starter-generator accessory belt reported to wear prematurely on early units
Hybrid system calibration - requires specialist diagnostics; not all rebuilders support K15C MHEV
entries.cta: Get quotes for K15C Suzuki Landy engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The Nissan-sourced MR20 powered the SHC26 Landy, offering smooth performance paired with CVT transmission.
compatibleVariants: SHC26 Landy 2.0G, SHC26 2.0X, SHC26 CVT variants
commonFailures: CVT transmission compatibility - engine replacement requires matching CVT history check
Oil consumption on high-mileage units - piston ring wear reported after 100k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for MR20 Suzuki Landy engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Diesel
entries.history: The D13A brought diesel efficiency to the Landy range but is known for high-pressure fuel pump vulnerabilities.
compatibleVariants: DA64W Landy 1.3 DDiS, DA64W Diesel 2WD, DA64W Diesel 4WD
commonFailures: HPFP failure - high-pressure fuel pump fails after 90k miles; TSB MZ-D13A-19 confirms widespread pattern
EGR cooler clogging - diesel particulate and carbon buildup reduces performance on short-journey use
entries.cta: Get quotes for D13A Suzuki Landy engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel DDiS
entries.history: The Renault-sourced F9Q DDiS offered robust torque for 4WD Landy variants but suffers EGR corrosion in UK conditions.
compatibleVariants: DA64W Landy 1.9 DDiS, DA64W Diesel 4WD high-spec variants
commonFailures: EGR cooler corrosion - UK road salt accelerates corrosion; stainless steel upgrade recommended
Turbo VGT actuator sticking - variable geometry turbo vanes carbon up, causing boost issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for F9Q Suzuki Landy engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Suzuki Landy Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Suzuki Landy's diverse engine range spans the Every Landy microvan (G13B) and MPV generations (M16A/M18A/K15B/K15C/D13A). Timing chain tensioner wear on M16A units and EGR carbon buildup on K15B engines dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: M16A Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why DA64W Landy Engines Rattle
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Landy DA64W 1.6G, 1.6X, JS, JX (2007-2016, M16A)
problems.rootCause: OEM timing chain tensioner (18210-85J00) loses hydraulic pressure, allowing chain slack and cold-start rattle; TSB SB-ENG-2013-07 confirms pattern.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner (18210-85J00), inspect chain guides, reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before chain stretch; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing chain kit, tensioner, guides, seals; engine partially stripped
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory spec; recommended if rattle present but no damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner, MLS gasket, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if head gasket failed or high-mileage; 12-month warranty included

problems.recommendation: On a DA64W Landy worth £3,000-£6,000, preventative tensioner replacement at 60k miles offers excellent value. If rattle is present but compression is good, the intermediate chain kit is sensible. If head gasket failure or high mileage (>120k) is confirmed, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components is often cheaper than major repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M16A engine prices for your Suzuki Landy DA64W

problems.h4: K15B EGR Carbon Buildup - Why DD51W Landy Engines Lose Performance
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Landy DD51W 1.5G, 1.5X, JS, JL, GLX (2016-2022, K15B)
problems.rootCause: EGR valve (13750-85Z00) accumulates carbon deposits from recirculated exhaust, restricting flow and causing rough idle, poor throttle response.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove EGR valve, ultrasonic clean, decarbonise intake manifold, reset ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; effective if caught early before severe coking

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new/reconditioned EGR valve (13750-85Z00), update seals, ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Longer-lasting solution; recommended if cleaning fails or valve seized

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with decarbonised EGR, updated valve seals, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if high-mileage (>100k) or multiple faults; 12-month warranty included

problems.recommendation: For DD51W Landy owners experiencing rough idle but otherwise healthy engines, EGR cleaning offers good short-term value. If the valve is seized or the car has covered 100k+ miles, investing in a rebuilt K15B with decarbonised components and updated seals often proves more cost-effective long-term.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K15B Suzuki Landy engine replacement with EGR upgrade

problems.h4: G13B Timing Belt Failure - Why Every Landy Engines Need Strict Maintenance
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Every Landy HE, HE22, 1.3G, 1.3X (1999-2005, G13B)
problems.rootCause: G13B is an interference engine; timing belt wear or snap causes pistons to strike open valves, resulting in catastrophic internal damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, water pump; reset service intervals
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure if done every 60k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded belt kit, piston rings if needed
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if belt snapped; rebuilt includes interference damage repair

problems.recommendation: On Every Landy microvans, preventative timing belt replacement every 60k miles is non-negotiable. If the belt has snapped, cylinder head damage is almost certain - a rebuilt G13B engine is typically cheaper and more reliable than attempting a head repair on a high-mileage unit.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt G13B engine prices for your Suzuki Every Landy

problems.h4: D13A High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - Why Landy Diesel Engines Won't Start
problems.rootCause: High-pressure fuel pump internal wear reduces rail pressure; TSB MZ-D13A-19 confirms premature failure pattern on UK-spec units.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace high-pressure fuel pump, prime system, ECU calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy and under 100k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated HPFP, injectors checked, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if high-mileage, injector damage suspected, or multiple faults

problems.recommendation: On a Landy DDiS with HPFP failure, assess injector condition first - failed pumps can send metal debris through the fuel system. If injectors are clean and mileage is under 100k, HPFP replacement may suffice. For higher-mileage units or suspected collateral damage, a rebuilt D13A with updated components offers better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt D13A diesel engine quotes for your Suzuki Landy

problems.h4: K15C ISG Belt Failure - Emerging Issue on New Hybrid Landy Models
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Landy DD52W Hybrid G, X, GLX (2022+, K15C Mild Hybrid)
problems.rootCause: Integrated starter-generator (ISG) accessory belt experiences higher load than conventional alternator belts; premature wear reported on early DD52W production.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace ISG belt, tensioner, inspect pulleys, reset hybrid system
repairOptions.longevity: First-line fix; effective if caught before ISG damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt K15C Hybrid Unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K15C with hybrid system calibration, updated belt kit
repairOptions.longevity: Required if ISG damaged; only specialists with MHEV expertise should handle

problems.recommendation: For DD52W Hybrid owners hearing belt noise or experiencing hybrid warning lights, early ISG belt replacement is cost-effective. Given the complexity of the mild-hybrid system, if ISG damage is confirmed, sourcing a rebuilt K15C from a specialist with MHEV calibration capability is essential - DIY or generic rebuilders cannot support this unit.
problems.cta: Enquire about K15C hybrid engine availability for your Suzuki Landy DD52W

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Suzuki Landy
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you own an Every Landy microvan or the latest DD52W Hybrid, engine problems don't have to mean a write-off. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK Suzuki Landy specialists who supply rebuilt, reconditioned and used engines with full warranty coverage. Compare prices, check supply & fit options, and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Suzuki Landy Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Suzuki Landy and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Suzuki Landy 1.3L Diesel Engines

fuelTypes.h2: Suzuki Landy Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Suzuki Landy engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the Every Landy G13B to the latest DD52W K15C hybrid.

items.title: Suzuki Landy Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol engines dominate the Landy range across all generations, from the Every Landy microvan (G13B) to the latest DD52W hybrid (K15C), with M16A/M18A powering the high-volume DA64W MPV generation.
items.descriptor: Petrol engines dominate the Landy range across all generations, from the Every Landy microvan (G13B) to the latest DD52W hybrid (K15C), with M16A/M18A powering the high-volume DA64W MPV generation.
items.familiesLabel: Common Suzuki Landy Petrol Engine Families
families: G13B - Every Landy microvan (1999-2005), interference engine, timing belt critical
M16A/M18A - DA64W Landy (2007-2016), timing chain tensioner and head gasket patterns
K15B/K15C - DD51W/DD52W Landy (2016+), EGR carbon and hybrid system considerations
MR20 - SHC26 Landy (2012-2014), Nissan-sourced, CVT compatibility check required
knownFor: M16A timing chain tensioner failures dominate DA64W replacement enquiries
K15B EGR carbon buildup common on urban-use DD51W units
G13B requires strict 60k-mile timing belt replacement (interference engine)
K15C hybrid requires specialist rebuild capability; limited supplier availability

items.title: Suzuki Landy Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel Landy options were limited to the DA64W generation (2008-2014), with D13A and F9Q engines offering economy but known HPFP and EGR corrosion issues.
items.descriptor: Diesel Landy options were limited to the DA64W generation (2008-2014), with D13A and F9Q engines offering economy but known HPFP and EGR corrosion issues.

items.descriptor: The K15C mild-hybrid system debuted on the DD52W Landy (2022+), adding an integrated starter-generator for efficiency and start-stop capability.

engineYears.h2: Suzuki Landy Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Suzuki Landy's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Every Landy microvan featured the G13B 1.3L petrol engine, an interference design requiring strict timing belt maintenance.
keyChanges: Launch of Every Landy microvan for Japanese domestic market; UK grey imports followed
G13B engine fitted across all HE/HE22 chassis variants
No diesel or hybrid options available in this generation
knownFor: G13B is an interference engine; timing belt failure causes catastrophic valve damage
Budget-friendly replacement option for import enthusiasts
Strict 60k-mile timing belt replacement interval critical
Low UK volumes mean specialist sourcing required
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2005 Suzuki Landy replacement engines
years.ticker: Suzuki Every Landy engine replacement • G13B timing belt failure • cheap Landy microvan engine UK

years.description: The rebadged Nissan Serena-based Landy launched with the M16A 1.6L petrol, introducing the timing chain tensioner issue that would dominate replacement enquiries.
keyChanges: Landy nameplate applied to Nissan Serena platform (DA64W chassis)
M16A 1.6L petrol became the volume engine; M18A 1.8L added for 4WD/high-spec
Diesel options (D13A, F9Q) introduced for UK grey import market
knownFor: M16A timing chain tensioner (18210-85J00) fails prematurely; TSB SB-ENG-2013-07 applies
Head gasket weakness on M16A/M18A; TSB ENG-08-03 confirms pattern
D13A diesel HPFP failures common after 90k miles (TSB MZ-D13A-19)
Highest-volume Landy generation in UK replacement market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2013 Suzuki Landy replacement engines
years.ticker: DA64W engine replacement • M16A timing chain tensioner • Landy head gasket failure UK

years.description: Late DA64W production saw increased M18A 1.8L fitment for 4WD variants, sharing the M16A's architecture with known oil pump relief valve issues.
keyChanges: M18A 1.8L became more common on 4WD and high-spec trims
Minor updates to emissions equipment; no major engine redesign
Diesel production continued but volumes declined
knownFor: M18A oil pump relief valve (15100-85J00) can stick, causing low oil pressure warnings
Head gasket pattern shared with M16A; MLS gasket upgrade recommended in rebuilds
Diesel units becoming harder to source as production wound down
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2016 Suzuki Landy replacement engines

years.description: The SHC26 Landy featured the Nissan-sourced MR20 2.0L petrol, offering smooth performance paired exclusively with CVT transmission.
keyChanges: SHC26 chassis introduced as premium Landy variant
MR20 2.0L petrol fitted across range; no diesel option
CVT transmission standard; verify history when replacing engine
knownFor: MR20 offers smooth power but oil consumption reported on high-mileage units
CVT transmission compatibility critical when replacing engine
Low UK volumes mean specialist sourcing and higher pricing
years.cta: Enquire about SHC26 Suzuki Landy engine availability

years.description: The rebadged Toyota Noah-based DD51W Landy introduced the K15B 1.5L petrol, bringing modern emissions compliance but EGR carbon challenges.
keyChanges: Landy nameplate moved to Toyota Noah platform (DD51W chassis)
K15B 1.5L petrol became the sole engine option; diesel discontinued
Updated emissions equipment; EGR valve (13750-85Z00) prone to carbon coking
knownFor: K15B EGR valve carbon buildup causes rough idle and performance loss
Intake manifold carbon deposits common on urban-use, high-mileage units
No diesel option from this generation onward
Rebuilt units include decarbonised EGR and updated valve seals
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2022 Suzuki Landy replacement engines
years.ticker: DD51W engine replacement • K15B EGR valve problem • Landy rough idle fix UK

years.year: 2022+ - DD52W Landy Third Generation (K15C Mild Hybrid)
years.description: The latest DD52W Landy introduced the K15C mild-hybrid system, adding an integrated starter-generator for efficiency but requiring specialist rebuild capability.
keyChanges: Landy moved to updated Toyota Noah platform (DD52W chassis)
K15C 1.5L mild-hybrid petrol became standard; no conventional petrol option
48V ISG system adds complexity; only specialists with MHEV expertise can rebuild
mainEngines: K15C - 1.5L Mild Hybrid Petrol (DD52W Hybrid G/X/GLX/4WD)
knownFor: K15C ISG belt wear reported on early production units (40-70k miles)
Hybrid system calibration requires specialist diagnostics and tools
Used K15C units not recommended due to system complexity
Rebuilt units include updated ISG belt and hybrid system verification

years.ticker: DD52W hybrid engine • K15C ISG belt failure • Landy mild hybrid replacement UK

faq.h2: Suzuki Landy Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing engine on Suzuki Landy?
items.answer: Yes - replacement is often more cost-effective than major repair on Landy engines. G13B rebuilt units from £750, M16A from £1,100, K15B from £1,600 typically undercut head gasket or HPFP repair bills while including warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing known failure points
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on most supplier units
Supply & fit options available nationwide through vetted UK specialists
Generation identification critical before ordering to avoid compatibility issues
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Suzuki Landy engine prices for your generation

items.question: How much to replace Suzuki Landy engine UK?
items.answer: Supply-only rebuilt engine prices range from £750 (G13B Every Landy) to £2,600 (K15C hybrid DD52W). Supply & fit adds £400-£900 labour depending on variant and workshop location.
keyPoints: G13B Every Landy: £750-£1,100 rebuilt (supply only)
M16A/M18A DA64W: £1,100-£1,700 rebuilt (supply only)
K15B DD51W: £1,600-£1,950 rebuilt (supply only)
K15C hybrid DD52W: £2,100-£2,600 rebuilt (supply only)
items.cta: Get tailored Suzuki Landy engine quotes with supply & fit options

items.question: Can I fit a M16A engine to my G13B Landy?
items.answer: No - the Every Landy (G13B) and MPV Landy (M16A) are completely different vehicles with incompatible chassis, engine mounts and ECU systems. Engines are not interchangeable between generations.
keyPoints: Every Landy uses HE/HE22 chassis; MPV Landy uses DA64W/DD51W/DD52W
Engine mounting points, wiring looms and ECU protocols differ completely
Always verify your chassis code before ordering any replacement engine
Use registration lookup to confirm exact generation and engine code match
items.cta: Identify your Suzuki Landy generation before ordering replacement engine

items.question: How long does Landy engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 2-5 working days after order confirmation. Supply & fit installations take 1-3 days depending on engine complexity and workshop schedule.
keyPoints: G13B/M16A/K15B units usually available from UK stock or fast JDM import
Hybrid K15C units may require longer lead time for specialist sourcing
Supply & fit bookings depend on specialist garage availability in your area
Allow extra time for ECU calibration on hybrid or late-model variants
items.cta: Check live Suzuki Landy engine availability and delivery timelines

items.question: What warranty comes with Landy replacement engine?
items.answer: Every rebuilt and reconditioned Suzuki Landy engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from the supplying specialist.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Terms vary slightly by supplier; confirm exact coverage before ordering
Keep service records and use recommended oil specs to maintain validity
Hybrid K15C warranty may require specialist calibration documentation
items.cta: Compare warranty terms across Suzuki Landy engine suppliers

items.question: Do you supply and fit Suzuki Landy engines?
items.answer: Yes - UK-wide supply & fit is available through our network of vetted Suzuki Landy specialists, with labour costs typically £400-£900 added to the engine price.
keyPoints: Supply & fit includes engine delivery, installation, fluids and warranty registration
Hybrid K15C units require fitters with MHEV calibration capability
Get multiple quotes to compare total installed cost across specialists
Nationwide coverage; rural locations may incur travel surcharges
items.cta: Find Suzuki Landy engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: What is difference between Every Landy and Landy?
items.answer: Every Landy (1999-2005) is a compact microvan with G13B engine; Landy (2007+) is a large MPV based on Nissan Serena/Toyota Noah platforms with M16A/K15B/K15C engines. They share only the nameplate.
keyPoints: Every Landy: HE/HE22 chassis, G13B 1.3L petrol, microvan body style
Landy MPV: DA64W/DD51W/DD52W chassis, multiple engine options, MPV body
Engines, parts and compatibility are NOT interchangeable between the two
Always confirm chassis code (HE vs DA64W vs DD51W) before ordering
items.cta: Verify your Suzuki Landy generation using registration lookup

items.question: Which Landy engine is most reliable?
items.answer: The M16A (with upgraded timing chain tensioner) and K15B (with decarbonised EGR) offer the best balance of reliability and parts availability for UK Landy owners.
keyPoints: M16A: Robust design; fit upgraded tensioner (18210-85J00) to prevent rattle
K15B: Modern emissions; regular EGR cleaning prevents carbon buildup
G13B: Reliable if timing belt replaced every 60k miles (interference engine)
Avoid high-mileage D13A diesel units due to HPFP failure risk
items.cta: Compare rebuilt M16A and K15B Suzuki Landy engine options

items.question: Will a JDM Landy engine work in my UK Landy?
items.answer: Yes - all Suzuki Landy engines are JDM-sourced, but you must match generation, chassis code and ECU specification. Hybrid K15C units require specialist calibration for UK use.
keyPoints: Confirm chassis code match: HE, DA64W, DD51W or DD52W
Verify engine code and variant compatibility before ordering
Hybrid K15C may require ECU recalibration for UK-spec systems
Use registration lookup to ensure exact match before purchase
items.cta: Get quotes for generation-matched Suzuki Landy replacement engines

items.question: How do I find my Landy engine code and generation?
items.answer: Check your chassis plate or VIN: HE/HE22 = Every Landy (G13B), DA64W = M16A/M18A, DD51W = K15B, DD52W = K15C hybrid. Engine code is stamped on the block near the gearbox.
keyPoints: Chassis code is the most reliable identifier for Landy generation
Engine code stamp location varies: G13B near timing cover, M/K-series near gearbox
DVLA registration lookup instantly confirms variant and engine details
Take photos of chassis plate and engine stamp when sourcing replacement
items.cta: Use registration lookup to identify your exact Suzuki Landy engine code
